title: An AI-based symptom checker integrated into MomConnect maternal healthcare platform: a prospective, observational mixed-methods study
methods: Bayesian network diagnostic decision support system
physician panel safety rating (exact binomial test, Clopper-Pearson CI)
composite barrier score (district health system + individual socioeconomic indicators)
Stuart-Maxwell test for paired categorical behaviour change
International Wealth Index via principal component analysis
thematic analysis of qualitative phone interviews
choropleth mapping with OCHA administrative boundaries
data_sources: Humanitarian Data Exchange - South Africa subnational boundaries (OCHA)
International Wealth Index / Global Data Lab
core_conclusion: Integrating a Bayesian-network DDSS into South Africa's government WhatsApp maternal health platform MomConnect was associated with clinically safe advice in 98.4% of 184 physician-reviewed cases and improved health-seeking behaviour: 44.6% of users changed care-seeking intentions, safe behaviours rose by 18.1 percentage points, and care-seeking prevalence climbed from 17.0% to 43.0% at one-week follow-up — even among the 86.3% facing multiple barriers to care. Income, not geography, was the primary predictor of failing to act safely on the advice received.
why_it_matters: First large-scale safety-rated evaluation of a patient-facing DDSS on a government LMIC platform; reaches high-barrier populations via low-data WhatsApp; demonstrates actual behaviour change not just intention shifts; quantifies income as the binding bottleneck; offers a policy-embedded, nationally scalable model.
why_nature: Mixed-methods rigor in a hard-to-study population; directly addresses SDG 3 maternal mortality target; bridges clinical AI safety and public health equity; demonstrates a government-industry collaboration model with applicability beyond South Africa.
added_value_to_my_research: Barrier composite score + IWI as covariates for GWR/MGWR mapping of mHealth effectiveness across SA provinces
Spatiotemporal KDE on care-contact events provides empirical distance-decay parameters for 2SFCA catchment delineation in LMIC settings
LLM fine-tuning on MomConnect interaction logs as a culturally grounded health AI for Sub-Saharan Africa
SHAP explainability applied to income × barrier interaction reveals spatially varying predictors of digital health non-compliance
Physician-panel evaluation protocol as reusable open benchmark for FAIR comparison of AI health tools in LMIC contexts

related.title: Access to care affects electronic health record reliability and AI-driven disease prediction

Choropleth maps of participant geographic distribution by South African province with provincial MMR, IWI, and composite barrier scores
